messages.content: I am working with a sensor vendor. They developed a proximity sensor that sends distance data regularly to our server. they use ESP32 with arduino. So I asked them to use crypto.h for hmac authentication for every data they send to our server in order to verify it's only authenticated device that sends data to our server. But the vendors are saying it's difficult to load a library in their board. and asking alternative using native code to authenticate. Is their request valid ?it doesn't make sense to me. Also I think hmac is the lightest authentication method I can provide. RSA or any jwt will involve more communication and data or memory
Can you suggest a lightweight or native solution

evidence: Can you suggest a lightweight or native solution
